sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content: There is currently no automated enforcement that documentation is current before a PR merges. Agents can mark nodes clean and run tests, but nothing prevents stale, unverified nodes from merging. There is also no structured artifact for a developer to review what the agent verified — the developer either has to trust the agent blindly or audit the index manually. The verification gate closes this loop: a CI check that fails on unresolved stale nodes, and a deterministic impact report that gives the developer everything they need to review in one place before approving the PR.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-VG-01 — CI gate on stale nodes**
As a developer, I want a GitHub action that fails if any stale nodes remain unverified so that documentation drift cannot merge without being addressed.

**US-VG-02 — Impact report for PR review**
As an agent, I want to generate a deterministic impact report summarizing all changes since the last merge — what changed, what was verified and by whom, what tests passed — so that the developer has a single artifact to review before approving the PR.

**US-VG-03 — Agent vs human distinction**
As a developer, I want agent-verified nodes to be clearly distinguished from human-verified nodes in the report so that I can see at a glance what was assessed automatically vs what a human reviewed.

**US-VG-04 — Mark clean with reason**
As an agent, I want to mark a stale node clean with a reason and my identity so that the impact report shows what I assessed and why — not just that verification happened.

**US-VG-05 — PR approval as human sign-off**
As a developer, I want the impact report to be the review artifact so that approving the PR serves as human sign-off on the batch without requiring per-node manual verification steps.

sections.heading: V1 Verification Model
sections.content:
The gate uses the **Impact Report + PR Approval** model.

1. Agent makes code changes, updates docs, runs tests, marks stale nodes clean with a reason (`verified_by='agent'`).
2. Agent runs `cortex report` — pure SQL read over `node_history`, `node_verification`, and `test_results`. No LLM. Deterministic and fast.
3. Impact report is attached to the PR. Developer reads it as part of code review and approves the PR. That approval is the human review of the batch.
4. GitHub action runs `cortex check --fail-on any` and verifies an impact report was generated. If either fails, the PR cannot merge.

**V1 gate behavior:** agent-verified is sufficient to pass. The `verified_by` field distinguishes `'agent'` from `'human'` in the database, but the gate does not require `'human'` in V1. This keeps the workflow moving while the agent system is being established. `--strict-human` mode (require human sign-off at node level) is V2.

**Reference point for "since":** The report needs a baseline — "what changed since when?" Three options, same as the viz "since" filter:
- `--since checkpoint` (default) — last CHECKPOINT row in history
- `--since <sha>` — a specific git commit (e.g. merge-base with main)
- `--since <date>` — an ISO timestamp

The same `GET /api/impact` endpoint serves both the CLI `cortex report` command and the viz "Generate Report" button.

**Impact report structure:**

```
# Cortex Impact Report
Since: checkpoint @ 2026-03-14 (git:a1b2c3d)
Generated: 2026-03-16T14:30:00Z
Project: cortex

## Summary
- 12 nodes changed (8 code, 2 docs, 2 new)
- 9 verified (7 agent, 2 human)
- 3 unverified (CONTENT_STALE)
- Tests: 45 passed, 0 failed

## Unverified (action required)
| Node | Change | Staleness | Last verified |
|---|---|---|---|
| get_meta() | CODE_ONLY ×2 | CONTENT_STALE | never |
| _parse_level3_lines() | CODE_ONLY | CONTENT_STALE | 2026-03-10 (agent) |

## Agent-verified
| Node | Change | Verified by | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| build() | CODE_AND_DESC | agent:claude-sonnet | "Added test_paths, docs updated" |
| upsert_node() | CODE_ONLY | agent:claude-sonnet | "Whitespace only" |

## Human-verified
| Node | Verified by | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| compute_staleness() | human | "Reviewed new LINKED_STALE logic" |

## New nodes (first discovered in this window)
- scan_module() @ cortex/scanners/module_scanner.py#L10-L45

## Test results
| Node | Test | Status | Duration |
|---|---|---|---|
| build() | test_build_idempotent | PASSED | 0.3s |
```

Sections are ordered by urgency: unverified first (action required), then agent-verified (review needed), then human-verified (done), then new nodes, then tests. A developer scanning top-down hits the action items first.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| `node_verification` table | Done |
| `cortex mark-clean` command | Done |
| `verified_by` field (`'agent'` / `'human'`) | Done |
| `reason` field in verification | Done |
| `code_hash_at` / `desc_hash_at` verification snapshots | Done |
| Doc sections verifiable | Done |
| `cortex check --fail-on` for CI | Done |
| `cortex report` CLI command (`--since-sha`, `--since`, `--format text/json`, `--change-type`, `--node`, `--node-type`, `--list-refs`) | Done |
| `cortex_report` MCP tool (same capabilities as CLI) | Done |
| `git_sha` populated in `node_history` during builds | Done |
| Batch `cortex_mark_clean` via `node_ids` param | Done |
| Shared `mark_node_clean` helper (`cortex/index/mark_clean.py`) | Done |
| Baseline hash reset on `nodes` table after mark-clean | Done |
| DocJSON section hashing in mark-clean (no more AST parse on JSON) | Done |
| Step 5 verification promotion produces CLEAN (not VERIFIED) | Done |
| GitHub Actions workflow | Not started |

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
**`cortex report`** — The core V1 deliverable. See the Verification Model section for the full content spec and example output. Outputs structured markdown ordered by urgency (unverified → agent-verified → human-verified → new → tests). Saved to `.cortex/report.md` and printed to stdout. Accepts `--since checkpoint` (default), `--since <sha>`, or `--since <date>` to set the reference point. The same data is served by `GET /api/impact` for the viz "Generate Report" button. Also outputs JSON (`--format json`) for machine consumption.

**GitHub Actions workflow** — A composable `cortex-gate` action that (1) runs `cortex check --fail-on any` and (2) checks that `.cortex/report.md` exists and was generated after the last commit. Configurable `project-root` input. V1 accepts agent-verified as passing.

**`--strict-human` gate mode** — Require `verified_by='human'` on all verifications for CI to pass. Useful once the agent workflow is mature and you want to enforce that a human personally reviewed every change. Adds a `--strict-human` flag to the GitHub action and to `cortex check`.

**Per-node human sign-off command** — `cortex verify <node_id>` for a developer to mark a specific node as human-verified from the CLI without going through the full mark-clean flow. Enables fine-grained sign-off without the PR approval being the only human touchpoint.

**Report diffing** — Compare two reports (current vs last merged) to show only what is net-new in this PR. Reduces noise when a repo accumulates a long history of agent-verified nodes.
